name: Mobile Usability
description: How the product listing should adapt to mobile devices to support efficient thumb-driven browsing and filtering

heuristics.principle: Filter access on mobile should use a full-screen drawer or modal rather than cramping filters into the main view
checkpoints: A clearly labelled filter button triggers a full-screen or slide-out panel
Filter drawer shows all filter options in a scrollable layout
Apply and clear buttons are visible within the filter drawer
Product count updates live within the filter drawer before applying

heuristics.principle: Touch targets for product cards, filters, and pagination should meet minimum size requirements for comfortable interaction
checkpoints: Product cards are large enough to tap without mis-taps
Filter checkboxes and toggles meet 44x44px minimum touch area
Pagination buttons or load-more are easily tappable
Swatches and small interactive elements have adequate spacing

heuristics.principle: The mobile product grid should prioritise product images and key info without excessive scrolling per product
checkpoints: Product cards show image, name, and price without scrolling within the card
Two-column grid provides a good balance of detail and density
Card content does not overflow or get clipped on narrow screens
Sticky header or sort/filter bar does not consume excessive screen space
